text: Some think the ‘tabernacle’ mentioned here and in 9.11 below is our Lord’s human body (cf. Mk. 14.58, Jn. 2.21); others, that it is the Church, either Triumphant (which accords best with 9.11) or Militant (which accords best with the use of the word ‘tabernacle’, or ‘tent’, applying as it does to what is temporary and transient, as in II Cor. 5.4).

‘Must needs have an offering to make’; that is, himself, cf. 7.27 above. Probably this is what the Apostle means by the pith of his argument (verse 1 above), viz. that Christ has only become our High Priest by dying and triumphing over death. Hence the reference to ‘if he were still on earth’ in the verse which follows.
